lenses.guidance: The chapter moves outward — altar (vv 1-8), courtyard (vv 9-19), lamp (vv 20-21). The narrative structure inverts ch 25-26's inward-from-ark sequence; entry comes by altar, presence comes by ark, light through both.

The bronze altar (vv 1-8) sits at the courtyard entrance — sacrifice precedes approach. The pattern matches the cross at Christianity's entrance: no path to presence that does not pass through atonement.

The lamp burns 'from evening until morning' continually (v 21) — covenant presence persists through every dark watch. The redemptive arc keeps a flame Israel cannot extinguish, prefiguring the unquenchable light of Christ.
